req.title: System-Admin deletes team
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to delete a team through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers a UI-driven team deletion flow for a system-admin. I derived: (1) a positive EP/STC case covering the happy path where a valid team is deleted and an audit record is written; (2) a negative NEG/DT case where an unauthenticated user is rejected with 401; (3) a boundary BVA case where a team whose name is exactly at the minimum allowed length (1 character) is deleted successfully; (4) a negative DT case where a forbidden (non-admin) role receives 403; (5) a negative NEG case triggering a 400 by attempting to delete with a missing/invalid team identifier. This gives full coverage of the error pathways and the happy path, satisfying the minimum three-test requirement while exercising EP, BVA, STC, DT, and NEG heuristics.

tests.name: test_admin_deletes_existing_team_successfully
preconditions: A team with known id exists (created via <fixture:existing_team>)
actions: Locate the target team row by id
Click the delete button for the team
Confirm the deletion in the confirmation dialog
Assert success response (200 or 201) with entity id
Assert team no longer appears in the team list
Assert audit record exists for the deletion event
expected: Response body contains the deleted team entity id
Team is absent from /teams list after deletion
Audit log contains one record referencing the team id and action=DELETE
trace: R-WEB-011: authenticated system-admin deletes a team
R-WEB-011: response 200 or 201 with entity id
R-WEB-011: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_admin_deletes_existing_team_successfully(page, admin_session, existing_team, audit_log_client):
    team_id = existing_team["id"]

    page.goto("/teams")
    row = page.locator(f"[data-testid='team-row-{team_id}']")
    row.locator("[data-testid='delete-btn']").click()
    page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete-btn']").click()

    response = page.wait_for_response(
        lambda r: "/teams" in r.url and r.request.method in ("DELETE", "POST")
    )
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"

    body = response.json()
    assert str(team_id) in str(body), "Entity id missing from response"

    page.reload()
    expect(page.locator(f"[data-testid='team-row-{team_id}']")).not_to_be_visible()

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=team_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit record not written"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_team
actions: Issue a DELETE (or POST) request to /teams/<team_id> without an auth token
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_team(base_url, existing_team, audit_log_client):
    team_id = existing_team["id"]
    url = f"{base_url}/teams/{team_id}"

    response = requests.delete(url, headers={}, timeout=10)

    assert response.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401 Unauthorized, got {response.status_code}"
    )

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=team_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, "Audit record must not be written for 401 case"

tests.name: test_admin_deletes_team_with_minimum_length_name
preconditions: A team whose name is exactly 1 character long exists (created via <fixture:min_name_team>)
actions: Locate the team row for the 1-character-named team
Assert response 200 or 201 with entity id
expected: Response body contains the deleted team entity id
Team with 1-character name is absent from list after deletion
Audit log contains a DELETE record for the team
trace: R-WEB-011: mandatory field validation - team name lower boundary (1 char)
R-WEB-011: response 200 or 201 with entity id
R-WEB-011: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_admin_deletes_team_with_minimum_length_name(page, admin_session, min_name_team, audit_log_client):
    team_id = min_name_team["id"]
    assert len(min_name_team["name"]) == 1, "Precondition: team name must be exactly 1 char"

    page.goto("/teams")
    row = page.locator(f"[data-testid='team-row-{team_id}']")
    row.locator("[data-testid='delete-btn']").click()
    page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete-btn']").click()

    response = page.wait_for_response(
        lambda r: "/teams" in r.url and r.request.method in ("DELETE", "POST")
    )
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"

    body = response.json()
    assert str(team_id) in str(body), "Entity id missing from response"

    page.reload()
    expect(page.locator(f"[data-testid='team-row-{team_id}']")).not_to_be_visible()

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=team_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit record not written for minimum-name-length team"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_team
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role=viewer (not admin)
actions: Issue a DELETE (or POST) request to /teams/<team_id> with a viewer-role auth token
trace: R-WEB-011: error pathway - 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_team(base_url, viewer_auth_headers, existing_team, audit_log_client):
    team_id = existing_team["id"]
    url = f"{base_url}/teams/{team_id}"

    response = requests.delete(url, headers=viewer_auth_headers, timeout=10)

    assert response.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403 Forbidden, got {response.status_code}"
    )

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=team_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, "Audit record must not be written for 403 case"

tests.name: test_delete_team_with_invalid_id_returns_400
actions: Issue a DELETE (or POST) request to /teams/ with a blank or invalid team id (e.g. empty string or non-numeric value)
expected: HTTP response status is 400 Bad Request
Response body contains a validation error message
trace: R-WEB-011: error pathway - 400 on validation error
R-WEB-011: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

@pytest.mark.parametrize("bad_id", ["", "not-a-valid-id", "!@#$"])
def test_delete_team_with_invalid_id_returns_400(base_url, admin_auth_headers, audit_log_client, bad_id):
    url = f"{base_url}/teams/{bad_id}"

    response = requests.delete(url, headers=admin_auth_headers, timeout=10)

    assert response.status_code == 400, (
        f"Expected 400 Bad Request for id={bad_id!r}, got {response.status_code}"
    )

    body = response.text
    assert body, "Response body should contain a validation error message"

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=bad_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, "Audit record must not be written for invalid id"
